blast_radius.no_other_consumers: grep-confirmed no other .py file in agents/ references normalize_email, EmailFields, or any Enron-specific symbol; agents/ingestion/dispatch.py (3.3.4) not yet built. Purely additive change, zero risk to existing 3.3.1 code or any other module.

risks.risk: Thread-id derivation is a judgment call (no native corpus field) and could be interpreted differently by a future 3.3.4/dispatch consumer or by a verifier.
risks.mitigation: Explicitly documented in module docstring and plan.md: primary signal is In-Reply-To/References when present (RFC-defensible, standard thread-linking headers); fallback is a normalized-subject-based grouping key (case-folded, Re:/Fwd:/Fw: prefixes stripped, whitespace collapsed) when those headers are absent, which is a documented, common approach in email-normalization/threading literature. Both paths are unit-tested.

risks.risk: Hand-authored fixtures might not faithfully represent real Enron corpus quirks (e.g. missing headers, folded header lines, non-UTF-8 bytes in real corpus).
risks.mitigation: Fixtures modeled directly on documented real Enron maildir header conventions (Message-ID, Date, From, To, Subject, Cc, Bcc, X-From, X-To, X-cc, X-bcc, X-Folder, X-Origin, X-FileName) per public knowledge of the corpus format. One fixture intentionally omits optional headers (Cc/Bcc/In-Reply-To) to test the corpus's common case. Real-corpus byte-level edge cases (charset issues) are out of scope for this subtask's acceptance criteria and better addressed under issue #19 (real dataset ingestion) if/when real files are staged.

out_of_scope_confirmed: Directory/corpus-level batch ingestion of a full maildir tree
Staging real Enron dataset files under data/ (issue #19)
